analysis.summary: Overall DQ on `loan1` slid from 0.847 to 0.812 (-0.035) across the Q1 window, a steady monthly decline rather than a one-off shock. Completeness in `customers` and Timeliness in `payments` are the dominant drivers; Validity and Uniqueness held firm.

overall_trend.narrative: Score moved from 0.847 to 0.812, a decline beyond the +/-0.02 stable band, with each monthly snapshot worse than the last.

key_findings: Completeness fell from 0.91 to 0.86 driven mainly by rising null rates on customers.credit_score (2.8% to 4.6%) and customers.phone (1.2% to 5.1%).
Payments accumulated 38 additional Late/Missed records vs. 2025-01-01, dragging Timeliness from 0.83 to 0.78.
Validity remained stable at 0.94 - format/range integrity is holding despite churn elsewhere.
Uniqueness held at 0.95+ across all four snapshots; surrogate-key discipline is intact.
The decline is monotonic month-over-month, indicating an upstream regression rather than a single-snapshot anomaly.
risks: If credit_score null rates continue rising, downstream credit-risk models will degrade and CCAR submissions will require manual remediation.
Late-payment growth signals genuine portfolio stress; SLA breaches and reserve top-ups are likely next quarter.
Without intervention, overall DQ projects below the 0.80 board-reporting floor by 2025-06-01.
recommendations: Patch the digital-channel onboarding flow to enforce non-null capture for customers.credit_score and customers.phone.
Tighten the Completeness rule on customers.credit_score to <= 3% null and route breaches to the data-domain owner via the dq_admin registry.
Stand up a weekly trend run instead of monthly to catch drift before it reaches the next regulatory cut.
Open a service-level review on the ACH posting job to address the payments timeliness regression at the source.

dimension_trends.narrative: Largest regression of the period; concentrated in customers.
Mild slip on cross-table date invariants.

table_trends.narrative: Null rates climbed on phone and credit_score.
Loans table is the resilient core of the portfolio.
